document: RBI Digital Lending Directions 2025 Digital Lending Guidelines 2022 (India - Row 14)

sections.heading: Preamble
sections.summary: Reserve Bank mandates credit system operation, encourages financial innovation while ensuring stability and depositor/borrower protection. Addresses concerns in digital lending.
sections.source_quote: Reserve Bank is statutorily mandated to operate the credit system of the country to its advantage.

sections.heading: Applicability
sections.summary: Applies to all digital lending activities of Commercial Banks, Primary Co-operative Banks, State Co-operative Banks, Central Co-operative Banks, Non-Banking Financial Companies, and All-India Financial Institutions.
sections.source_quote: These Directions shall be applicable to all digital lending activities of the following entities, hereinafter referred to as a Regulated Entity (RE) and collectively as Regulated Entities (REs), as the context may require

sections.heading: Definitions
sections.summary: Defines key terms including Annual Percentage Rate, Default Loss Guarantee, Digital Lending, Digital Lending Apps/Platforms, and Lending Service Providers.
sections.source_quote: For the purpose of these Directions, unless the context otherwise requires, the terms herein shall bear the meaning assigned to them below

sections.heading: Due diligence requirements with respect to LSPs
sections.summary: RE must have contractual agreements with LSPs defining roles, conduct enhanced due diligence, perform periodic reviews, establish monitoring mechanisms, and ensure LSP compliance with regulations.
sections.dtia_summary: RE must conduct enhanced due diligence on LSPs, perform periodic reviews, and ensure LSP compliance with regulations.

requirements.text: RE shall conduct enhanced due diligence before entering into agreement with LSP for digital lending
RE shall carry out periodic review of LSP conduct against contractual terms
RE shall lay down monitoring mechanisms for loan portfolios originated with LSPs
RE shall ensure LSPs comply with applicable regulations

sections.source_quote: RE shall conduct enhanced due diligence before they enter into an agreement with a LSP for digital lending

sections.heading: RE-LSP arrangements involving multiple lenders
sections.summary: LSPs must provide transparent loan offers to borrowers, match lenders fairly, disclose loan details objectively, and avoid dark patterns in digital lending platforms.
sections.dtia_summary: LSPs must provide transparent loan offers, match lenders fairly, disclose loan details objectively, and avoid dark patterns.

requirements.text: LSP shall provide digital view of all loan offers matching borrower's request
LSP shall follow consistent approach for similarly placed borrowers
Digital view shall include name of REs, loan amount, tenor, APR, repayment obligations
Content displayed by LSP shall be unbiased and objective

sections.source_quote: LSP shall provide a digital view of all the loan offers matching the borrower's request on the DLA

sections.heading: Assessing the borrower's creditworthiness
sections.summary: RE must obtain borrower's economic profile (age, occupation, income) for creditworthiness assessment and maintain records for audit purposes.
sections.dtia_summary: RE must obtain borrower's economic profile for credit assessment and maintain records for audit.

requirements.text: RE shall obtain necessary information relating to borrower's economic profile

sections.source_quote: RE shall obtain the necessary information relating to economic profile of the borrower with a view to assessing the borrower's creditworthiness

sections.heading: Disclosures to borrowers
sections.summary: RE must provide Key Fact Statement (KFS), ensure fair penal charges, digitally sign documents, maintain public website with product details, and communicate recovery agent details.
sections.dtia_summary: RE must provide KFS, ensure fair penal charges, digitally sign documents, maintain public website with product details, and communicate recovery agent details.

requirements.text: RE shall provide Key Fact Statement (KFS) as per circular instructions
RE shall ensure digitally signed documents flow to borrower via email/SMS
RE shall maintain public website with product details, LSPs, grievance mechanisms, and privacy policies
RE shall communicate recovery agent details to borrower via email/SMS

sections.source_quote: RE shall provide a Key Fact Statement (KFS), as per instructions contained in circular no. DOR.STR.REC.13/13.03.00/2024-25

sections.heading: Loan disbursal, servicing and repayment
sections.summary: RE must disburse loans directly to borrower's bank account, ensure no third-party pass-through accounts, and maintain direct fund flow between borrower and RE.
sections.dtia_summary: RE must disburse loans directly to borrower's bank account, ensure no third-party pass-through accounts, and maintain direct fund flow.

requirements.text: Disbursement of loan shall always be made into borrower's bank account
All loan servicing and repayment must be executed directly in RE's bank account
Flow of funds between borrower and RE shall not be controlled by third party

sections.source_quote: Disbursement of loan by the RE shall always be made into the bank account of the borrower

sections.heading: Cooling-off period
sections.summary: Borrowers must have explicit option to exit loan during cooling-off period (minimum 1 day) with principal and proportional APR without penalty.
sections.dtia_summary: Borrowers must have explicit option to exit loan during cooling-off period (minimum 1 day) with principal and proportional APR without penalty.

requirements.text: Borrower shall be given explicit option to exit loan by paying principal and proportional APR without penalty during cooling-off period

sections.source_quote: The borrower shall be given an explicit option to exit a digital loan by paying the principal and the proportionate APR without any penalty during an initial 'cooling-off period'

sections.heading: Grievance redressal
sections.summary: RE and LSP must designate nodal grievance redressal officers, display contact details prominently, and provide complaint mechanisms including RBI's CMS portal.
sections.dtia_summary: RE and LSP must designate nodal grievance redressal officers, display contact details prominently, and provide complaint mechanisms including RBI's CMS portal.

requirements.text: RE and LSP shall designate nodal grievance redressal officers
Contact details of nodal officers shall be prominently displayed on websites and KFS
Borrowers can lodge complaints via RBI's CMS portal or physical complaint to RBI

sections.source_quote: The RE, and the LSP which has an interface with the borrower, shall designate nodal grievance redressal officers

sections.heading: Collection, usage and sharing of data with third parties
sections.summary: RE must ensure data collection is need-based with explicit consent, restrict data sharing with third parties, and provide options for data control and deletion.
sections.dtia_summary: RE must ensure data collection is need-based with explicit consent, provide options for data control and deletion, and restrict data sharing with third parties.

requirements.text: RE shall ensure data collection by DLA is need-based with prior and explicit consent
Borrower shall be provided option to give/deny consent for data use, restrict disclosure, and revoke consent
Purpose of obtaining consent must be disclosed at each stage of interface
Explicit consent required before sharing personal information with third parties

sections.source_quote: RE shall ensure that any collection of data by their DLA and DLA of their LSP is need-based and with prior and explicit consent of the borrower

sections.heading: Storage of data
sections.summary: RE must ensure LSPs store only minimal borrower data (name, address, contact) and retain data within India with 24-hour deletion for cross-border processing.
sections.dtia_summary: Requires minimal data storage, India-only servers, 24-hour data deletion for cross-border processing, and clear data retention policies.

requirements.text: RE must ensure LSPs store only minimal borrower data (name, address, contact) for operations
RE must ensure data stored only in India servers
RE must ensure data deleted from servers outside India within 24 hours of processing
RE must ensure clear policy on data storage duration, usage restrictions, and destruction protocols

sections.source_quote: RE shall ensure that LSP engaged by them do not store personal information of borrower except some basic minimal data (viz., name, address, contact details of the customer, etc.) that may be required to carry out their operations or service within the scope of the RE-LSP agreement.

sections.heading: Comprehensive privacy policy
sections.summary: RE and LSPs must have publicly available comprehensive privacy policies disclosing third parties allowed to collect personal information.
sections.dtia_summary: Requires public privacy policies for RE and LSPs that disclose third parties collecting personal information.

requirements.text: RE and LSPs must have comprehensive privacy policies compliant with applicable laws
Privacy policies must disclose third parties allowed to collect personal information

sections.source_quote: RE and LSPs engaged by RE shall have a comprehensive privacy policy compliant with applicable laws, associated regulations and RBI guidelines which shall be made available publicly on the website of RE and LSP, as the case may be.

sections.heading: Technology standards
sections.summary: RE and LSPs must comply with RBI and other relevant agencies' cybersecurity technology standards for digital lending.
sections.dtia_summary: Requires RE and LSPs to follow RBI's cybersecurity standards for digital lending operations.

requirements.text: RE and LSPs must comply with RBI and other relevant agencies' cybersecurity technology standards

sections.source_quote: RE shall ensure that they and the LSPs engaged by them comply with various technology standards/ requirements on cybersecurity stipulated by RBI and other relevant agencies, or as may be specified from time to time, for undertaking digital lending.

sections.heading: Reporting to Credit Information Companies (CICs)
sections.summary: RE must report all digital lending activities to Credit Information Companies (CICs) regardless of loan type or tenor.
sections.dtia_summary: Requires reporting of all digital lending products to CICs for credit information purposes.

requirements.text: RE must report all digital lending activities to CICs irrespective of nature/tenor
RE must ensure LSPs comply with outsourcing guidelines for deferred payment credit products

sections.source_quote: RE shall ensure that any lending done through their DLAs and/or DLAs of LSPs is reported by them to CICs irrespective of its nature/ tenor.

sections.heading: Reporting of DLAs to RBI
sections.summary: RE must report all DLAs to RBI's CIMS portal with details on data storage compliance and grievance redressal mechanisms.
sections.dtia_summary: Requires reporting of DLAs to RBI with data storage compliance and grievance redressal details.

requirements.text: RE must report all DLAs deployed to RBI's CIMS portal
RE must update DLAs list on CIMS portal as new DLAs are deployed or existing ones cease
Chief Compliance Officer must certify DLAs comply with data storage and privacy policies
RE must ensure DLAs have link to website with loan product details and privacy policies
RE must ensure DLAs have nodal grievance redressal officer for borrower complaints
RE must ensure data collection and storage comply with data storage regulations

sections.source_quote: RE shall report all DLAs deployed/ joined by them, whether their own or those of the LSPs, either exclusively or as a platform participant, on the Centralised Information Management System (CIMS) portal of RBI in the requisite format as given in the Annex-I to these Directions.

sections.heading: Due diligence and other requirements with respect to DLG provider
sections.summary: RE must establish Board-approved policy for DLG providers including eligibility criteria, coverage, monitoring, and fee details.
sections.dtia_summary: Requires Board-approved policy for DLG providers with eligibility criteria, coverage, monitoring, and fee details.

requirements.text: RE must put in place Board-approved policy for DLG arrangements
Policy must include eligibility criteria for DLG providers
Policy must specify nature and extent of DLG cover
Policy must include process for monitoring and reviewing DLG arrangements
Policy must detail fees payable/received by DLG providers

sections.source_quote: RE, including a RE acting as DLG provider, shall put in place a Board approved policy before entering into any DLG arrangement. Such policy shall include, at a minimum, the eligibility criteria for DLG provider, nature and extent of DLG cover, process of monitoring and reviewing the DLG arrangement, and the details of the fees, if any, payable to/ received by the DLG provider, as the case may be.

sections.heading: Restrictions on entering into DLG arrangements
sections.summary: RE cannot enter into DLG arrangements for revolving credit facilities, credit cards, or loans covered by credit guarantee schemes.
sections.dtia_summary: Restricts DLG arrangements for revolving credit facilities, credit cards, and loans covered by credit guarantee schemes.

requirements.text: RE shall not enter into DLG arrangements for revolving credit facilities offered through digital lending channel and credit cards
RE shall not enter into DLG arrangements on loans covered by credit guarantee schemes administered by trust funds

sections.source_quote: RE shall not enter into DLG arrangements for revolving credit facilities offered through digital lending channel and credit cards as defined under Master Direction – Credit Card and Debit Card – Issuance and Conduct Directions, 2022 dated April 21, 2022.

sections.heading: Treatment of DLG for regulatory capital
sections.summary: DLG providers that are REs must deduct full DLG amount outstanding from their regulatory capital.
sections.dtia_summary: Requires REs acting as DLG providers to deduct full DLG amount from regulatory capital.

requirements.text: REs acting as DLG providers must deduct full DLG amount outstanding from regulatory capital

sections.source_quote: In case, DLG provider is an RE, it shall deduct full amount of the DLG which is outstanding from its capital.

sections.heading: Disclosure requirements
sections.summary: RE must disclose monthly to LSPs the total number of portfolios and amounts on which DLG has been offered.
sections.dtia_summary: Requires monthly disclosure of DLG portfolio details to LSPs.

requirements.text: RE must ensure LSPs publish monthly total portfolios and amounts with DLG
Disclosures must be made within 7 working days after month-end

sections.source_quote: RE shall put in place a mechanism to ensure that LSPs with whom they have a DLG arrangement shall publish on their website the total number of portfolios and the respective amount of each portfolio on which DLG has been offered.

sections.heading: Exceptions
sections.summary: DLG for certain credit guarantee schemes and international institutions are excluded from DLG definition.
sections.dtia_summary: Excludes DLG for credit guarantee schemes and international institutions from DLG definition.

requirements.text: DLG covered under CGTMSE, CRGFTLIH, and NCGTC schemes are excluded
DLG provided by BIS, IMF, and multilateral development banks are excluded

sections.source_quote: Guarantees covered under the following schemes/ entities shall not be covered within the definition of DLG: [list of schemes]

sections.heading: General provisions
sections.summary: EMI programs on credit cards are governed by specific Master Directions and not covered by this regulation.
sections.dtia_summary: EMI programs on credit cards are governed by specific Master Directions and not covered by this regulation.

requirements.text: EMI programs on credit cards are governed by specific Master Directions

sections.source_quote: RBI/2019-20/258 DOR(NBFC)(PD) CC.No.112/03.10.001/2019-20 24.06.2020 Loans Sourced by Banks and NBFCs over Digital Lending Platforms: Adherence to Fair Practices Code and Outsourcing Guidelines
